speaker: PHILO
text: Nay, but this dotage of our general’s

speaker: PHILO
text: O’erflows the measure. Those his goodly eyes,

speaker: PHILO
text: That o’er the files and musters of the war

speaker: PHILO
text: Have glowed like plated Mars, now bend, now turn

speaker: PHILO
text: The office and devotion of their view

speaker: PHILO
text: Upon a tawny front. His captain’s heart,

speaker: PHILO
text: Which in the scuffles of great fights hath burst

speaker: PHILO
text: The buckles on his breast, reneges all temper

speaker: PHILO
text: And is become the bellows and the fan

speaker: PHILO
text: To cool a gypsy’s lust.

speaker: PHILO
text: Look where they come.

speaker: PHILO
text: Take but good note, and you shall see in him

speaker: PHILO
text: The triple pillar of the world transformed

speaker: PHILO
text: Into a strumpet’s fool. Behold and see.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If it be love indeed, tell me how much.

speaker: ANTONY
text: There’s beggary in the love that can be reckoned.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll set a bourn how far to be beloved.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Then must thou needs find out new heaven, new Earth.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: News, my good lord, from Rome.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Grates me, the sum.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nay, hear them, Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Fulvia perchance is angry. Or who knows

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If the scarce-bearded Caesar have not sent

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: His powerful mandate to you: “Do this, or this;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Take in that kingdom, and enfranchise that.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Perform ’t, or else we damn thee.”

speaker: ANTONY
text: How, my love?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Perchance? Nay, and most like.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: You must not stay here longer; your dismission

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is come from Caesar. Therefore hear it, Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where’s Fulvia’s process? Caesar’s, I would say— both?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Call in the messengers. As I am Egypt’s queen,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou blushest, Antony, and that blood of thine

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is Caesar’s homager; else so thy cheek pays shame

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: When shrill-tongued Fulvia scolds. The messengers!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let Rome in Tiber melt and the wide arch

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of the ranged empire fall. Here is my space.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Kingdoms are clay. Our dungy earth alike

speaker: ANTONY
text: Feeds beast as man. The nobleness of life

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is to do thus; when such a mutual pair

speaker: ANTONY
text: And such a twain can do ’t, in which I bind,

speaker: ANTONY
text: On pain of punishment, the world to weet

speaker: ANTONY
text: We stand up peerless.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Excellent falsehood!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Why did he marry Fulvia and not love her?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll seem the fool I am not. Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will be himself.

speaker: ANTONY
text: But stirred by Cleopatra.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now for the love of Love and her soft hours,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let’s not confound the time with conference harsh.

speaker: ANTONY
text: There’s not a minute of our lives should stretch

speaker: ANTONY
text: Without some pleasure now. What sport tonight?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hear the ambassadors.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fie, wrangling queen,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whom everything becomes—to chide, to laugh,

speaker: ANTONY
text: To weep; whose every passion fully strives

speaker: ANTONY
text: To make itself, in thee, fair and admired!

speaker: ANTONY
text: No messenger but thine, and all alone

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tonight we’ll wander through the streets and note

speaker: ANTONY
text: The qualities of people. Come, my queen,

speaker: ANTONY
text:
Last night you did desire it.
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Messenger
                .
              Speak not to us.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Is Caesar with Antonius prized so slight?

speaker: PHILO
text: Sir, sometimes when he is not Antony

speaker: PHILO
text: He comes too short of that great property

speaker: PHILO
text: Which still should go with Antony.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I am full sorry

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: That he approves the common liar who

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Thus speaks of him at Rome; but I will hope

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Of better deeds tomorrow. Rest you happy!
